summary: Published ops.example.com and changelog.example.com live through the shared NGINX edge, moved the edge certificate flow to Hetzner-backed DNS-01 validation, and verified both generated portal surfaces locally on nginx-lv3.

verification.observed: The Hetzner authoritative zone and a direct `dig @1.1.1.1` lookup both resolved `ops.example.com` and `changelog.example.com` to `203.0.113.1`.
The live `lv3-edge` certificate on nginx-lv3 now includes `DNS:ops.example.com` and `DNS:changelog.example.com` alongside the existing edge hostnames.

verification.check: Portal HTTPS responses on the edge VM
verification.observed: Host-header HTTPS probes against `127.0.0.1` on nginx-lv3 returned `HTTP/2 200` for both `ops.example.com` and `changelog.example.com` after the edge converge.

notes: The initial `playbooks/provision-subdomain.yml` run exposed a controller-defaults lookup bug; the repo now includes the playbook fix and regression coverage.

The original webroot ACME flow could not complete new hostname expansion because HTTP-01 reached persistent external connection timeouts, so the shared edge role now uses a pinned `certbot-dns-hetzner` plugin in an isolated virtualenv for DNS-01 validation.

The `ops` and `changelog` A records were created directly through the Hetzner DNS API before the final converge because the singular record playbook create path was still failing under Ansible during the first live attempt.
